tags.name: Wrack Lina
tags.qa_note: Near-duplicate of Lina wreck (osm_id 495589635). Both entries refer to the same vessel. Kept both as OSM has two distinct nodes. Built 1879 Andrew Leslie shipyard, 70m long, 1049 GRT, sank 14 Jan 1914 on fog near Cres island.

tags.name: Grotte Vrbnik
tags.qa_note: Entry type corrected from boat to shore — dive centers confirm cave is 100m from dive center, accessed directly. Difficulty corrected to Beginner per dive center descriptions. Cave extends ~35m interior, chimney to surface, entrance at 13m.

tags.name: Peltastis
tags.qa_note: Difficulty upgraded to Advanced — requires AOW or equivalent, 7 lives lost in sinking. Depth range 15-32m. Greek cargo vessel (original name Alsterpark, built Kremer Sohn shipyard Germany 1952). Sank Jan 7-8 1968 in Bora storm, hit cliffs between Šilo and Klimno. Sources conflict on exact length: OSM tags say 60.2m, elpi-tours says 60m, dive center says 70m — likely 60m is correct (OSM sourced from wreck records). Sources conflict on casualties: 7-8 reported.

tags.name: Vajavine
tags.qa_note: Entry type corrected from boat to shore — dive centers confirm this is their house reef accessed by shuttle bus/ladder. Depth corrected to 30m+ (sources list 30m+ max). Known locally as '100 Steps' (100 stairs to the sea, dive ladder entry).

tags.name: Silent Beach
tags.qa_note: Entry type corrected from boat to shore — confirmed as house reef accessible by shuttle bus from Vrbnik dive centers. Difficulty corrected to Beginner — described as flat entry, beginner-friendly by both dive centers.

tags.name: Secret beach
tags.qa_note: Entry type corrected from boat to shore — confirmed as house reef accessed via small cave to hidden beach, 'few meters' from dive center. Difficulty corrected to Beginner — described as shallow training area. Drop to 30m wall is one aspect, but entry area is beginner-friendly.

tags.name: El Hawi Star Wrack
tags.qa_note: Difficulty upgraded to Advanced (42m, expert-tagged in OSM). Confirmed: built 1957 Hamburg as M/B Valeria, renamed El Hawi Star 1981, sank Dec 12 1982 after improper cargo loading of stone slabs and lumber, ~500m from Excelsior breakwater near Rijeka. Lies on starboard side 26-42m.

tags.description: swimming and underwater sightseeing in shallow water
tags.name: podmorska poučna staza Tripuljak
tags.qa_note: Entry type corrected from boat to shore — OSM tag describes this as a shallow underwater trail at 2m depth, clearly shore-accessible. Cannot confirm 3 independent sources. Reverted to unvalidated.

tags.name: Francesca da Rimini
tags.qa_note: Date conflict: OSM says 24.01.1944; Tramonto Diving (primary dive operator) says 22.03.1944 (March 22, 1944). Updated wreck:date_sunk to 1944 only (year confirmed, exact date conflicted). Vessel: Italian cargo warship built 1942 Rijeka, 42m long, 281 tons, seized by Germans 1943. Sank near Kaprije island, torpedoed by British Spitfires. Depth 38-50m.

tags.name: Wrack Ribolovc
tags.qa_note: Previously marked validated=true with source=perplexity. Cannot confirm 3 independent sources for this specific site. OSM entry says 'boat or shore'. Reverted to unvalidated.

tags.name: Plavi Jadran
tags.qa_note: Confirmed: 30m cargo ship Plavi Jadran sank 31 Jan 2018 after collision with Jadrolinija ferry Tijat near Prvić island. Depth 20-22m. Both crew survived. Deliberately accessible beginner wreck. Location near Prvić island (not just 'near Šibenik' as previous markdown stated).

tags.name: Stuka / Junkers Ju 87
tags.qa_note: Aircraft is Italian Air Force Ju 87 R-2 (NOT German aircraft as implied in previous markdown). Crashed/forced down April 12 1941 near Žirje island during attack on Yugoslav warships at Jadrovac near Šibenik. Discovered Sept 2014. Declared Croatian cultural heritage. Depth 28m. Diving requires licensed dive center escort. Previous text incorrectly stated aircraft was 'shot down by anti-aircraft defense' — sources indicate it was forced to ditch, crew believed to have escaped (no remains found). Engine found separately at ~40m.
